the large sidewheeler of the St. Joe
Transportation company, which is the
auxiliary of the Occur d'Alene & Spo-
kane electric line, the Colfax and the
Spokane. All of these boats except
the one belonging to the st. Joe did
a large business last summer. The St
Joe company's boat was not com.
pleted until the fall, but it is one OF
the largest on the lake and the owners
had planned for it to do much of the
business of the lake and river.


Captain of the Spokane, the well
known lake pilot, is in the city to-
day. Said the captain:


81 do not look for the transportation
business to get its full swing until
about June, but the outlook for the
coming summer from a business stand :
point is exceptionally bright. The
building of the new electric line will
greatly add to the business of Occur
d'Alene city, Idaho, and of course
when things are lively there it helps
our business. l don't know what !
shall do in case sell my boat"

pany will employ the present skip
pers in running the business.


Lake Attractions.


General Manager Belts of the Occur
d'Alene & Spokane Electric line says
it will be early in the summer before
the trains will make the run from
this city to Occur d'Alene in one hour
as the track will hardly be in shape
and properly ballasted until then. The
Washington Water Power company is
about ready to put in the sub-electri-
cal stations necessary to give the in
creased power to make such a run. 1t
is not determined just yet what special
attractions will be provided by the
company for the summer travel. 1t is
said park will be fixed with dane
ing pavilion and music of several sorts
and the resort made ideal in every
way for the summer excursionist.
